title: PV Decision Trees
copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. Decision tree logic and chain topology are proprietary.
description: Atomic pharmacovigilance decision trees (micrograms): sub-microsecond PV logic for signal classification, causality routing, seriousness assessment, and regulatory action. Chains compose multiple micrograms into complete workflows.

tools.name: run-pv-signal-to-action
tools.description: Run the core PV workflow chain: PRR signal detection → causality bridge → Naranjo assessment → regulatory action routing. Returns step-by-step decision trace with final regulatory recommendation.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: Proportional Reporting Ratio value

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo ADR probability score (0-13)

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: Whether the adverse event meets ICH E2A seriousness criteria

final_output.description: Accumulated chain output with regulatory_action, deadline_days, causality, priority

tools.name: run-case-assessment-pipeline
tools.description: Run the complete case assessment chain: case validity → seriousness classification → causality evidence → regulatory action.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value for the drug-event pair

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo score (0-13)

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: ICH E2A seriousness criteria met

parameters.name: reporter_type
parameters.description: Reporter type: healthcare_professional, consumer, literature

parameters.name: has_rechallenge
parameters.description: Was rechallenge performed

tools.name: run-benefit-risk-assessment
tools.description: Run the benefit-risk assessment chain: weighs therapeutic benefit against safety risk using PRR, Naranjo, NNH, and therapeutic index.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo score

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: Seriousness flag

parameters.name: therapeutic_benefit
parameters.description: Benefit level: high, moderate, low

parameters.name: nnh
parameters.description: Number needed to harm

tools.name: run-prr-signal
tools.description: Classify a PRR value as signal/no-signal using Evans (2001) criteria. PRR >= 2.0 = signal.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value to classify

tools.name: run-naranjo-quick
tools.description: Quick Naranjo causality assessment from a score. Returns causality category (DEFINITE/PROBABLE/POSSIBLE/DOUBTFUL) with confidence and recommended action.

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo score (0-13)

tools.name: run-case-seriousness
tools.description: Classify case seriousness per ICH E2A criteria. Returns seriousness category, expedited reporting requirement, and deadline.

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: Meets ICH E2A seriousness criteria

parameters.name: is_fatal
parameters.description: Resulted in death

parameters.name: is_life_threatening
parameters.description: Life-threatening event

parameters.name: requires_hospitalization
parameters.description: Required or prolonged hospitalization

tools.name: run-workflow-router
tools.description: Route a PV case to the appropriate workflow based on case characteristics. Returns workflow type, priority, and first action.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo score

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: Seriousness flag

parameters.name: signal_detected
parameters.description: Signal detection result

tools.name: list-micrograms
tools.description: List all available micrograms with their descriptions and test counts.

tools.name: list-chains
tools.description: List all available microgram chains with their step sequences.

tools.name: run-station-dailymed-pipeline
tools.description: DailyMed ADR label data → risk tier → causality routing. Pipe get-adverse-reactions output fields directly into this chain to get a risk classification and monitoring decision.

parameters.name: has_boxed_warning
parameters.description: Drug has an FDA boxed warning

parameters.name: has_rems
parameters.description: Drug is subject to a Risk Evaluation and Mitigation Strategy (REMS)

parameters.name: serious_reactions_count
parameters.description: Number of serious adverse reactions listed in the label

parameters.name: total_reactions_count
parameters.description: Total number of adverse reactions listed in the label

tools.name: run-station-pubmed-pipeline
tools.description: PubMed signal literature → evidence strength → causality routing. Pipe search-signal-literature output into this chain to get an evidence-based signal classification.

parameters.name: total_articles
parameters.description: Total article count from literature search

parameters.name: case_report_count
parameters.description: Number of case reports in search results

parameters.name: systematic_review_count
parameters.description: Number of systematic reviews or meta-analyses

parameters.name: recent_count
parameters.description: Number of articles published in the last 3 years

parameters.name: has_regulatory_citation
parameters.description: Any article cites a regulatory safety action (label change, withdrawal, REMS)

tools.name: run-station-openvigil-pipeline
tools.description: OpenVigil disproportionality → signal triage → causality routing. Pipe compute-disproportionality output fields into this chain to get a signal strength classification and causality decision.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: Proportional Reporting Ratio

parameters.name: prr_ci_lower
parameters.description: Lower bound of 95% CI for PRR

parameters.name: ror
parameters.description: Reporting Odds Ratio

parameters.name: ror_ci_lower
parameters.description: Lower bound of 95% CI for ROR

parameters.name: ic
parameters.description: Information Component value

parameters.name: case_count
parameters.description: Total drug-event pair report count

tools.name: run-station-trial-pipeline
tools.description: Clinical trial SAE data → safety concern triage → causality routing. Pipe compare-trial-arms or get-serious-adverse-events output into this chain to get a DSMB-level safety decision.

parameters.name: treatment_sae_rate
parameters.description: SAE rate per 100 patients in treatment arm

parameters.name: control_sae_rate
parameters.description: SAE rate per 100 patients in control arm

parameters.name: total_saes
parameters.description: Total serious adverse event count in trial

parameters.name: has_deaths
parameters.description: Any treatment-arm deaths reported

parameters.name: phase
parameters.description: Trial phase: 1, 2, 3, or 4

tools.name: run-station-drugbank-pipeline
tools.description: DrugBank DDI data → interaction severity → causality routing. Pipe get-interactions output into this chain to get a clinical decision for drug-drug interaction management.

parameters.name: interaction_count
parameters.description: Total drug-drug interactions found

parameters.name: cyp_inhibition
parameters.description: Any CYP enzyme inhibition interaction present

parameters.name: narrow_ti_involved
parameters.description: Any narrow therapeutic index drug involved in an interaction

tools.name: run-station-rxnav-pipeline
tools.description: RxNav drug interaction data → interaction severity → signal routing. Pipe get-interactions output into this chain to classify the clinical significance of identified interactions.

parameters.name: interaction_count
parameters.description: Total interactions found via RxNorm

parameters.name: has_contraindication
parameters.description: Any contraindicated drug pair present

parameters.name: has_serious
parameters.description: Any serious (non-contraindicated) interaction present

parameters.name: has_moderate
parameters.description: Any moderate interaction present

tools.name: run-station-recall-pipeline
tools.description: FDA recall data → recall severity classification → regulatory action routing. Pipe search-recalls or get-recall-classification output into this chain to determine urgency of safety response.

parameters.name: recall_class
parameters.description: FDA recall class: I (most serious), II, or III

parameters.name: is_ongoing
parameters.description: Recall is currently active and ongoing

parameters.name: distribution_scope
parameters.description: Recall distribution scope: nationwide, regional, or local

tools.name: run-seriousness-to-deadline
tools.description: ICH E2A seriousness classification → boolean decomposition → deadline routing. Input the 6 ICH E2A seriousness criteria flags to get the case classification, deadline in days, and reporting obligation.

parameters.name: death
parameters.description: Adverse event resulted in or contributed to death

parameters.name: hospitalization
parameters.description: Required inpatient hospitalization or prolongation of existing hospitalization

parameters.name: disability
parameters.description: Caused persistent or significant disability or incapacity

parameters.name: life_threatening
parameters.description: Patient was at immediate risk of death at time of event

parameters.name: congenital_anomaly
parameters.description: Congenital anomaly or birth defect in offspring

parameters.name: medically_important
parameters.description: Medically important event per ICH E2A criteria

deadline_days.description: Reporting deadline in days (7 for fatal/life-threatening, 15 for other serious, null for non-serious)

tools.name: run-adr-severity-escalation
tools.description: ADR CTCAE grading → escalation routing. Classify adverse drug reaction severity using CTCAE-aligned criteria and get the appropriate escalation pathway.

parameters.name: is_fatal
parameters.description: ADR was fatal (CTCAE Grade 5)

parameters.name: is_life_threatening
parameters.description: ADR was life-threatening (CTCAE Grade 4)

parameters.name: requires_hospitalization
parameters.description: ADR required hospitalization (CTCAE Grade 3)

parameters.name: requires_medical_intervention
parameters.description: ADR required medical intervention (CTCAE Grade 2)

tools.name: run-confidence-deadline
tools.description: Assessment confidence level → reporting urgency classification. Given an assessment confidence score and days until reporting deadline, routes to the appropriate urgency tier.

parameters.name: confidence
parameters.description: Assessment confidence as a percentage (0-100)

parameters.name: deadline_days
parameters.description: Days remaining until regulatory reporting deadline

tools.name: run-investigation-prioritization
tools.description: Transbeyesian class propagation → EIG priority ranking. For a drug-event signal, determines whether to investigate directly or rely on propagated evidence from structurally similar drugs in the same class.

parameters.name: source_prr
parameters.description: PRR value for the source drug-event pair

parameters.name: signal_threshold
parameters.description: PRR threshold for signal detection (typically 2.0)

parameters.name: transfer_weight
parameters.description: Structural similarity class: same_class | high | moderate | low | none

parameters.name: prior_strength
parameters.description: Prior evidence strength: strong | moderate | weak | unknown

parameters.name: uncertainty_level
parameters.description: Current uncertainty: very_high | high | moderate | low

parameters.name: extremity_level
parameters.description: Signal extremity: extreme | moderate | low

parameters.name: propagation_count
parameters.description: Number of adjacent drugs in the class network that would benefit from this investigation

tools.name: run-signal-deep-validation
tools.description: Multi-dimensional signal quality assessment. Combines 4 disproportionality measures (PRR/ROR/IC/EBGM), validates criteria sufficiency, detects trend direction, and identifies recurrence patterns.

parameters.name: prr_signal
parameters.description: PRR signal classification: STRONG | MODERATE | WEAK | NONE

parameters.name: ror_signal
parameters.description: ROR signal classification: STRONG | MODERATE | WEAK | NONE

parameters.name: ic_signal
parameters.description: IC signal classification: STRONG | MODERATE | WEAK | NONE

parameters.name: ebgm_signal
parameters.description: EBGM signal classification: STRONG | MODERATE | WEAK | NONE

parameters.name: prr_above_threshold
parameters.description: PRR exceeds detection threshold (≥2.0)

parameters.name: case_count_sufficient
parameters.description: Case count meets minimum threshold (≥3)

parameters.name: geographic_spread
parameters.description: Reports come from ≥2 distinct geographic regions

parameters.name: temporal_pattern
parameters.description: Reports show a consistent temporal pattern (not clustered in one period)

parameters.name: prr_current
parameters.description: Current PRR value for trend detection

parameters.name: pct_change
parameters.description: Percent change in PRR from prior period (negative = declining)

parameters.name: occurrence_count
parameters.description: Total historical occurrences of this signal

parameters.name: prior_action_taken
parameters.description: A regulatory or clinical action was previously taken for this signal

tools.name: run-bradford-hill-evidence
tools.description: Bradford Hill evidence assessment: signal comparison → dose-response classification → Naranjo causality. A 3-criterion causality chain using cross-variable comparison micrograms.

parameters.name: signal_a
parameters.description: PRR or signal strength for the drug of interest

parameters.name: signal_b
parameters.description: PRR or signal strength for a comparator drug in the same class

parameters.name: low_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at low dose

parameters.name: mid_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at mid dose

parameters.name: high_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at high dose

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo ADR probability score (0-13)

tools.name: run-bradford-hill-multi-criterion
tools.description: Full 5-criterion Bradford Hill causality assessment: temporal association → signal comparison → dose-response → rechallenge → Naranjo. The most comprehensive single-call causality evaluation in the platform.

parameters.name: drug_start_to_event_days
parameters.description: Days from drug initiation to adverse event onset

parameters.name: event_before_drug
parameters.description: Event occurred before drug was started (dechallenge violation)

parameters.name: signal_a
parameters.description: PRR or signal strength for the drug of interest

parameters.name: signal_b
parameters.description: PRR or signal strength for comparator drug

parameters.name: threshold
parameters.description: Signal detection threshold (typically 2.0)

parameters.name: low_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at low dose

parameters.name: mid_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at mid dose

parameters.name: high_dose_rate
parameters.description: Adverse event rate per 100 patients at high dose

parameters.name: baseline_rate
parameters.description: Background rate of this event in the general population per 100 patients

parameters.name: first_exposure_rate
parameters.description: Event rate per 100 patients on first drug exposure

parameters.name: rechallenge_rate
parameters.description: Event rate per 100 patients on re-exposure after dechallenge

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo ADR probability score (0-13)

criteria_met.description: Number of Bradford Hill criteria satisfied (0-5)

tools.name: run-crystalbook-4primitive
tools.description: Crystalbook 4-Primitive Conservation Check: assess whether a system conserves Existence (∃), Boundary (∂), State (ς), and Nothing (∅). Returns per-primitive status and overall conservation diagnosis (CONSERVING/STRESSED/LOSING_COHERENCE/NON_EXISTENT). Source: Crystalbook v2.0 by Matthew A. Campion, PharmD.

parameters.name: existence_evidence
parameters.description: Evidence that the system exists — observable artifacts, running processes, measurements

parameters.name: boundary_description
parameters.description: Description of the system's boundaries — where it begins and ends, what's inside vs outside

parameters.name: state_observation
parameters.description: Observed current state — measured, not inferred from memory or assumption

parameters.name: void_identification
parameters.description: What is absent or missing — named voids that should be explored or filled

conservation_holds.description: True if all 4 primitives are healthy

tools.name: run-crystalbook-8law
tools.description: Crystalbook 8-Law Risk Matrix: assess virtue scores (0-10) for each of the 8 Laws of System Homeostasis. Scores 7+ = SATISFIED, 4-6 = AT_RISK, 0-3 = VIOLATED. Returns per-law status, violation count, and overall health (HEALTHY/GUARDED/DEGRADED/CRITICAL). Source: Crystalbook v2.0 by Matthew A. Campion, PharmD.

parameters.name: humility_score
parameters.description: Law I (Pride→Humility) virtue score 0-10. Does the system calibrate against reality?

parameters.name: charity_score
parameters.description: Law II (Greed→Charity) virtue score 0-10. Does the system circulate what it holds?

parameters.name: chastity_score
parameters.description: Law III (Lust→Chastity) virtue score 0-10. Does the system honor its commitments?

parameters.name: kindness_score
parameters.description: Law IV (Envy→Kindness) virtue score 0-10. Does the system strengthen its neighbors?

parameters.name: temperance_score
parameters.description: Law V (Gluttony→Temperance) virtue score 0-10. Does the system ingest only what it can metabolize?

parameters.name: patience_score
parameters.description: Law VI (Wrath→Patience) virtue score 0-10. Does the system correct proportionally?

parameters.name: diligence_score
parameters.description: Law VII (Sloth→Diligence) virtue score 0-10. Does the system maintain its maintenance function?

parameters.name: independence_score
parameters.description: Law VIII (Corruption→Independence) virtue score 0-10. Are boundaries resourced independently?

tools.name: run-crystalbook-diagnostic
tools.description: Crystalbook Full Diagnostic: combines 4-primitive conservation check + law triage into a single diagnostic. Returns conservation status, law assessment, overall verdict (HEALTHY/GUARDED/STRESSED/DEGRADED/CRITICAL/NON_EXISTENT), and a plain-English prescription. Source: Crystalbook v2.0 by Matthew A. Campion, PharmD.

parameters.name: existence_evidence
parameters.description: Evidence the system exists

parameters.name: boundary_description
parameters.description: Boundary definition

parameters.name: state_observation
parameters.description: Observed current state

parameters.name: void_identification
parameters.description: Named absences

parameters.name: humility_score
parameters.description: Law I virtue score 0-10

parameters.name: charity_score
parameters.description: Law II virtue score 0-10

overall_verdict.description: HEALTHY | GUARDED | STRESSED | DEGRADED | CRITICAL | NON_EXISTENT

prescription.description: Plain-English recommendation for restoring system health

tools.name: run-ich-q1a-stability-testing
tools.description: ICH Q1A(R2) Stability Testing decision tree. Given product type, storage type, and accelerated study outcome, returns storage conditions, testing frequency, minimum study duration, and shelf life approach. Covers general case, refrigerator, freezer, semi-permeable, and impermeable containers.

parameters.name: product_type
parameters.description: drug_substance or drug_product

parameters.name: storage_type
parameters.description: general, refrigerator, freezer, below_minus_20, semi_permeable, impermeable

parameters.name: long_term_condition
parameters.description: For general: 25_60 or 30_65. For semi-permeable: 25_40 or 30_35.

parameters.name: significant_change_accelerated
parameters.description: Whether significant change was observed at accelerated condition

parameters.name: significant_change_timing
parameters.description: within_3_months or between_3_and_6_months

tools.name: run-ich-q1b-photostability
tools.description: ICH Q1B Photostability Testing decision tree. Encodes the Decision Flow Chart: directly exposed → immediate pack → marketing pack → redesign. For drug substances, routes forced degradation/confirmatory results.

parameters.name: product_type
parameters.description: drug_substance or drug_product

parameters.name: confirmatory_result
parameters.description: DS only: photostable, photolabile, or equivocal

parameters.name: immediate_pack_impenetrable
parameters.description: DP only: true if pack is light-impenetrable (aluminum tubes, cans)

parameters.name: directly_exposed_acceptable
parameters.description: DP: whether change is acceptable when directly exposed

parameters.name: immediate_pack_acceptable
parameters.description: DP: whether change is acceptable in immediate pack

parameters.name: marketing_pack_acceptable
parameters.description: DP: whether change is acceptable in marketing pack

tools.name: run-ich-e1-population-exposure
tools.description: ICH E1 Population Exposure decision tree. Routes minimum safety database size for drugs intended for long-term treatment of non-life-threatening conditions. Standard: 1500 total, 300-600 at 6 months, 100 at 12 months. Handles 5 exception categories and regional variants (US priority, Japan).

parameters.name: treatment_duration
parameters.description: long_term (>6 months) or short_term

parameters.name: condition_severity
parameters.description: non_life_threatening or life_threatening

parameters.name: has_exception
parameters.description: Whether an exception condition applies

parameters.name: exception_type
parameters.description: late_developing_ade, low_frequency_ade, small_benefit, background_morbidity, small_population

parameters.name: region
parameters.description: us, eu, japan

parameters.name: is_priority_drug
parameters.description: US priority drug designation

tools.name: run-heligram
tools.description: Helix Computing decision tree — encode any system through the conservation law ∃ = ∂(×(ς, ∅)). Given boundary sharpness (∂), state richness (ς), void clarity (∅), classifies existence strength, identifies weakest primitive, routes to Crystalbook laws, and gates helix advancement.

parameters.name: boundary
parameters.description: ∂ — boundary sharpness [0,1]. How sharply defined is inside vs outside?

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: ς — state richness [0,1]. How much observable state exists?

parameters.name: void
parameters.description: ∅ — void clarity [0,1]. How clearly defined is what the system is NOT?

weakest.description: The weakest primitive: boundary, state, void, or none

can_advance.description: Whether the system can advance to the next helix turn

vice_risk.description: Capital vices that threaten this system

binding_laws.description: Which Crystalbook laws govern this system

tools.name: run-sota-drift-detection
tools.description: Guardian SOTA Tracker — detect frontier movement in a pharmacovigilance domain. Classifies the PV domain (signal detection, causality, benefit-risk, case processing, regulatory), evaluates a finding against the current frontier using evidence level and authority weight, and reports drift severity with recommended action. Returns threshold authority (who defines SOTA), comparison method, drift velocity, and frontier movement classification (PARADIGM_SHIFT, SIGNIFICANT, INCREMENTAL, NONE, UNQUALIFIED).

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: PV domain: signal_detection, causality, benefit_risk, case_processing, regulatory

parameters.name: evidence_level
parameters.description: Strength of evidence: 1=opinion, 2=case series, 3=observational, 4=RCT, 5=systematic review/meta-analysis

parameters.name: authority_weight
parameters.description: Threshold authority recognition: 1=preprint, 2=peer-reviewed, 3=guideline-cited, 4=regulatory-adopted, 5=ICH/WHO standard

parameters.name: novelty_score
parameters.description: Novelty vs current frontier: 0=known, 25=incremental, 50=significant, 75=paradigm-adjacent, 100=paradigm-shift

parameters.name: prr_change_pct
parameters.description: Observed PRR change percentage for drift severity classification (0 if not applicable)

parameters.name: time_window_days
parameters.description: Time window in days over which the change was observed (90 if not applicable)

threshold_authority.description: Who adjudicates SOTA in this domain

comparison_method.description: How findings are ranked against the frontier

drift_velocity.description: How fast the frontier typically moves

frontier_movement.description: PARADIGM_SHIFT, SIGNIFICANT, INCREMENTAL, NONE, or UNQUALIFIED

severity.description: Drift severity from CRITICAL_DRIFT to STABLE

action.description: Recommended response to the frontier finding

tools.name: run-sota-authority-decompose
tools.description: Decompose threshold authority into primitives — consensus breadth, credential strength, evidence basis, institutional backing — and score defensibility. Detects Takeda-pattern authority assertions (claims without defensible criteria). Returns authority class (SOVEREIGN/ESTABLISHED/EMERGING/CONTESTED/LONE/UNGROUNDED/ASSERTION), defensibility rating, vulnerability analysis, and recommendation.

parameters.name: consensus_level
parameters.description: Breadth of agreement: 1=single author, 2=research group, 3=professional society, 4=multi-society, 5=international harmonization (ICH/WHO)

parameters.name: credential_strength
parameters.description: Credentialing depth: 1=self-declared, 2=peer-recognized, 3=board-certified, 4=regulatory-designated, 5=statutory mandate

parameters.name: evidence_basis
parameters.description: Evidence grounding: 1=assertion only, 2=case-based, 3=empirical study, 4=validated framework, 5=replicated multi-site

parameters.name: institutional_backing
parameters.description: Institutional power: 1=individual, 2=academic dept, 3=professional body, 4=national regulator, 5=supranational (WHO/ICH/EMA)

authority_class.description: SOVEREIGN, ESTABLISHED, EMERGING, CONTESTED, LONE, UNGROUNDED, or ASSERTION

defensibility.description: MAXIMUM, HIGH, MODERATE, LOW, MINIMAL, or NONE

vulnerability.description: Specific weakness of this authority type

recommendation.description: How to handle this authority level

tools.name: run-sota-pubmed-pipeline
tools.description: PubMed → SOTA pipeline: triage a publication for frontier relevance, classify its PV domain, check frontier movement, and assess drift severity. Feed article metadata from a PubMed search and get back a full SOTA drift assessment including whether the finding warrants frontier review. Chain: pubmed-triage → domain-classifier → frontier-check → drift-alert.

parameters.name: article_type
parameters.description: PubMed article type: meta_analysis, systematic_review, rct, observational, case_series, case_report, editorial, letter

parameters.name: pub_year
parameters.description: Publication year (e.g., 2026)

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: PV domain: signal_detection, causality, benefit_risk, case_processing, regulatory

parameters.name: novelty_score
parameters.description: Novelty vs current frontier: 0=known, 25=incremental, 50=significant, 75=paradigm-adjacent, 100=paradigm-shift

parameters.name: citation_count
parameters.description: Number of citations (0 if new)

parameters.name: is_guideline_cited
parameters.description: 1 if cited in a guideline/regulatory document, 0 otherwise

parameters.name: prr_change_pct
parameters.description: Observed PRR change percentage (0 if not applicable)

parameters.name: time_window_days
parameters.description: Observation time window in days (90 if not applicable)

tools.name: run-caso-clinico-espanol
tools.description: Pipeline completo de evaluación de caso clínico en español. Grammar disambiguation (ser/estar + indicativo/subjuntivo) → temporal classification → epistemic weighting → ICH E2A seriousness → expectedness. 7-step chain preserving Spanish enrichment alongside standard ICSR outputs.

parameters.name: forma_verbal
parameters.description: Ambiguous verb form to disambiguate (e.g. 'presente', 'cause')

parameters.name: verbo
parameters.description: Ser/estar form: es/está/era/estaba/fue/estuvo etc.

parameters.name: sintoma
parameters.description: Síntoma clínico en español / Clinical symptom

parameters.name: verbo_contexto
parameters.description: Governing verb (creer/dudar/negar/esperar) for presente disambiguation

parameters.name: modo_verbal
parameters.description: Verb mood indicator: indicativo/subjuntivo or conjugated form (tiene/tenga)

parameters.name: death
parameters.description: ICH E2A: death occurred

parameters.name: hospitalization
parameters.description: ICH E2A: hospitalization required

parameters.name: disability
parameters.description: ICH E2A: persistent disability

parameters.name: life_threatening
parameters.description: ICH E2A: life-threatening

parameters.name: congenital_anomaly
parameters.description: ICH E2A: congenital anomaly

parameters.name: medically_important
parameters.description: ICH E2A: medically important event

parameters.name: event_in_label
parameters.description: Is the event listed in the drug label?

parameters.name: event_class_in_label
parameters.description: Is the event class listed in the label?

parameters.name: severity_in_label
parameters.description: Is the observed severity listed in the label?

tools.name: run-gramatica-causalidad-espanola
tools.description: Cadena de enriquecimiento de causalidad desde gramática española. Ser/estar temporal classification + indicativo/subjuntivo epistemic weighting. Returns temporality (TRANSITORIO/PERMANENTE) and confidence (ALTA/BAJA) for causality enrichment.

parameters.name: verbo
parameters.description: Ser/estar form: es/está/era/estaba etc.

parameters.name: sintoma
parameters.description: Clinical symptom in Spanish

parameters.name: modo_verbal
parameters.description: Verb mood: indicativo/subjuntivo or conjugated form

tools.name: run-presente-desambiguacion
tools.description: Desambigua formas verbales españolas entre indicativo y subjuntivo usando el verbo rector como árbitro. Resolves the presente/presenta collision where identical surface forms map to different grammatical moods.

parameters.name: forma_verbal
parameters.description: Ambiguous verb form (e.g. 'presente', 'cause', 'tenga')

parameters.name: verbo_contexto
parameters.description: Governing verb: creer/dudar/negar/esperar/afirmar/saber etc.

tools.name: run-disproportionality-consensus
tools.description: Consensus signal classifier across all 4 disproportionality measures (PRR, ROR, IC, EBGM). Counts how many methods detect a signal using standard thresholds and classifies as STRONG (4/4), MODERATE (3/4), WEAK (2/4), MARGINAL (1/4), or NONE (0/4). Feed pre-computed scores from compute_disproportionality_table or OpenVigil.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value (signal threshold: >= 2.0)

parameters.name: ror_ci_lower
parameters.description: ROR lower 95% CI (signal threshold: > 1.0)

parameters.name: ic025
parameters.description: IC lower 95% credibility interval (signal threshold: > 0)

parameters.name: eb05
parameters.description: EBGM 5th percentile (signal threshold: > 1.0)

parameters.name: n_cases
parameters.description: Number of reported cases (minimum 3 required)

tools.name: run-signal-consensus-to-action
tools.description: End-to-end chain: 4-measure disproportionality consensus → signal-to-causality routing → Naranjo causality assessment → regulatory action with ICH E2B deadlines. Takes pre-computed disproportionality scores + Naranjo score + seriousness flag. Returns consensus classification, causality level, and regulatory action with deadline.

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value (signal threshold: >= 2.0)

parameters.name: ror_ci_lower
parameters.description: ROR lower 95% CI (signal threshold: > 1.0)

parameters.name: ic025
parameters.description: IC lower 95% credibility interval (signal threshold: > 0)

parameters.name: eb05
parameters.description: EBGM 5th percentile (signal threshold: > 1.0)

parameters.name: n_cases
parameters.description: Number of reported cases (minimum 3 required)

parameters.name: naranjo_score
parameters.description: Naranjo ADR probability score (0-13)

parameters.name: is_serious
parameters.description: Whether the adverse event meets ICH E2A seriousness criteria

tools.name: run-anti-vector-pipeline
tools.description: Signal-to-prescription pipeline: PRR signal detection → anti-vector classification. Takes a PRR value and harm type, returns signal verdict plus the anti-vector strategy (primary class, measures, biases). Completes the sentence from 'there is a signal' to 'here is what to do about it.'

parameters.name: prr
parameters.description: PRR value for signal detection (threshold: >= 2.0)

parameters.name: harm_type
parameters.description: Harm type: A (Acute), B (Cumulative), C (Off-Target), D (Cascade), E (Idiosyncratic), F (Saturation), G (Interaction), H (Population)

parameters.name: signal_magnitude
parameters.description: Normalized signal magnitude 0.0-1.0 (optional, used for proportionality tier)

tools.name: run-microgram
tools.description: Run any microgram by name with JSON input. Exposes the full 1,384-program fleet. Returns decision path, output variables, and execution time (sub-microsecond).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Microgram name (e.g. prr-signal, naranjo-quick, case-seriousness)

tools.name: run-chain
tools.description: Run an ad-hoc microgram chain by expression. Each step output feeds the next. Example: "prr-signal -> signal-to-causality -> naranjo-quick".

parameters.name: chain
parameters.description: Chain expression: microgram names joined by " -> "

parameters.name: accumulate
parameters.description: If true, preserve all upstream outputs (default: true)

tools.name: run-helix-system-health
tools.description: Run the helix system health chain: heligram verification → crystalbook 4-primitive check → crystalbook 8-law validation. Returns layered health assessment.
